human_verification.notes: Reviewed the chemicals-plastics-and-materials-manufacturing customer-technical-services package for specialty boundary quality, authority-source quality, complaint and field-evidence handling, permit and inspection dependency coverage, escalation realism, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to chemicals customer technical-services work with SDS, TDS, COA, complaint, lab, field, permit, inspection, maintenance, and calibration-aware handling.

Authority sources cover APQC and ISO complaint workflow references, OSHA hazard communication and process-safety boundaries, EPA permit and hazardous-waste inspection guidance, and ISO asset-management evidence posture with refresh and decay rules.

Evaluation scenarios cover policy exceptions, missing prerequisites, permit and regulated evidence gaps, changed field conditions, engineering and safety signoff refusal, handoffs, repeat failures, unsupported workarounds, terminology handling, source conflicts, and tenant adaptation.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/chemicals-plastics-and-materials-manufacturing/customer-technical-services-specialist/ to keep this overlay in the canonical NAICS-derived path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around off-spec approval, engineering signoff, permit interpretation, waste classification, environmental judgment, and commercial exception authority.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated chemicals customer-technical-services overlay where engineering, EHS, regulatory, maintenance release, legal, and commercial approval authority remain external.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
